Title: Molecular Controls of Proanthocyanidin Synthesis and Structure: Prospects for Genetic Engineering in Crop Plants
Journal: Journal of Agricultural and Food Chemistry

Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Not specified; perspective discusses use of tissue-specific or constitutive promoters in engineering but no single promoter experimentally used in a focal system
Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Various species including Arabidopsis thaliana, Vitis vinifera, Populus spp., Medicago trunculata, Diospyros kaki, Malus domestica, Trifolium arvense, Medicago sativa
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: MATE transporters are useful companion genes for engineering efficient vacuolar sequestration of PA precursors; likely need stacking with PA biosynthetic and regulatory genes to ensure accumulation and avoid cytotoxicity.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Facilitates vacuolar transport and accumulation of PA precursors, supporting PA deposition.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transport assays in yeast membranes; characterization of PA-deficient mutants in Arabidopsis and Medicago trunculata
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Not a main focus; PA accumulation discussed across tissues and species
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Associated with PA-accumulating tissues such as seed coat and other PA-producing tissues
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Transporters preferentially move epicatechin-3'-O-glucoside; vacuolar polymerization mechanism remains unresolved.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May be required to avoid toxicity from ectopic PA biosynthesis; no direct tradeoff experimentally summarized here.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PA accumulation occurs in the vacuole and requires transport of precursors. 2. PA-related MATE transporters from Arabidopsis and Medicago preferentially transport epicatechin-3'-O-glucoside. 3. These transporters are enabling components for PA accumulation and likely useful in engineering.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Vacuole of seed coat, leaves, bark, roots, flowers, fruit, and other PA-accumulating tissues
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Human and animal health benefits; contributes to astringency, herbivore resistance, nutrient cycling, and anti-adhesion activity for some PA types

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Arabidopsis; Medicago; general plant PA pathway
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: ANR is a core enzyme for increasing epicatechin-derived PA synthesis; should be stacked with upstream flux genes/regulators and transport components for effective PA engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces anthocyanidins to 2,3-cis-flavan-3-ols such as epicatechin
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides the biosynthetic route to cis-flavan-3-ol precursors needed for PA synthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis of Arabidopsis banyuls (BAN); molecular characterization of the encoded enzyme
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate flavan-3-ol precursor for PA polymer assembly
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: PA-producing tissues such as seed coat and other PA-accumulating tissues
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Downstream branch step toward PA monomer formation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Key discovery established a route to cis-flavan-3-ols, complementing the LAR route to trans-flavan-3-ols.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Not specified directly; altered monomer balance may influence PA structure.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key branch enzyme for PA-specific precursor formation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BAN encodes ANR, a PA-specific enzyme. 2. ANR converts anthocyanidins to cis-flavan-3-ols required for PA biosynthesis. 3. Together with LAR, ANR defines the monomer precursor supply for PA assembly.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Seed coat and other PA-storing tissues
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Affects health-promoting properties, astringency, defense, and ecological functions

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: LAR can be used to alter catechin/epicatechin composition and potentially polymer length, but overexpression alone has not predictably generated desired PA structures; should be deployed cautiously and likely with polymerization-control genes once known.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces leucoanthocyanidins to 2,3-trans-flavan-3-ols such as catechin; additionally cleaves S-cysteinyl-epicatechin to release catechin
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supplies trans-flavan-3-ol precursors and may regulate PA polymer length by processing a proposed polymerization intermediate.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Enzyme assays; loss-of-function analysis in Medicago; metabolite/polymer characterization
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms monomer precursor and modulates polymerization state of end product
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: PA-producing tissues; expression/activity differences proposed to influence subunit ratios
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Reported in Medicago trunculata PA tissues
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Downstream branch step and polymerization-associated side control step
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Loss of LAR increases PA polymerization and reduces extractability, indicating a noncanonical role in controlling polymer length.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression may not yield predicted PA structure because of pathway complexity and competing activities such as ANS-mediated conversion.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: (+)-Catechin and related trans-flavan-3-ols; catechin released from S-cysteinyl-epicatechin
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key branch enzyme; also proposed regulator of polymer length
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species: Various plants; polymerization role highlighted in Medicago
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LAR provides the route to trans-flavan-3-ol precursors for PAs. 2. Medicago LAR also cleaves S-cysteinyl-epicatechin, releasing catechin. 3. Loss of LAR increases polymerization and lowers extractability, implicating LAR in PA polymer length control.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: PA-accumulating tissues including seed coat, leaves, fruit
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Shapes PA composition, extractability, and potentially bioavailability-related polymer length

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Vitis vinifera; Vaccinium spp.; Populus spp.; Rosa spp.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A prime engineering target for shifting PAs toward prodelphinidin-rich, more highly hydroxylated structures; likely requires coexpression with cytochrome b5 and consideration of DFR substrate specificity.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates the flavonoid B-ring at the 3' and 5' positions to generate trihydroxylated intermediates leading to gallocatechin/epigallocatechin-derived PA subunits
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Determines degree of B-ring hydroxylation and thereby PD:PC ratio in PAs.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression-metabolite correlation in grape and blueberry; transgenic overexpression observations in poplar; prior engineering in rose with DFR
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Diverts flux toward prodelphinidin-type PA subunits
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Tissue context such as grape berry skin versus seed affects interpretation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High in grape berry skin with high PD PAs; low in grape seed and blueberry tissues with low PD content
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: As above in grape and blueberry
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Overexpressing F3'5'H alone in poplar did not increase PD, suggesting cytochrome b5 limitation; downstream DFR specificity also matters.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: B-ring hydroxylation branch leading to prodelphinidins
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Structural modification of PAs may alter taste/astringency and interact with downstream enzyme specificities.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Early flavonoid substrates with incompletely hydroxylated B-rings
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Branch-switch enzyme for PA hydroxylation pattern
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species: Various plants including grape and blueberry
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. F3'5'H controls B-ring trihydroxylation and PD formation. 2. High expression correlates with PD-rich PAs in grape skin, whereas low expression correlates with PD-poor tissues. 3. Effective engineering likely requires coexpression with cytochrome b5 and compatible downstream enzymes.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Berry skin, seeds, fruit tissues, leaves depending on species
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Affects astringency, herbivore resistance, and nutrient cycling

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Grape; poplar and other plants with F3'5'H activity
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Should be coexpressed with F3'5'H to successfully elevate prodelphinidin content during PA engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Acts as electron-transfer cofactor required for efficient F3'5'H activity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports flavonoid B-ring trihydroxylation and elevated PD content.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression correlation with high PD content in grape skin; transgenic poplar correlation with increased PD content
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supports branch flux toward prodelphinidin-rich PAs
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Tissue context such as grape skin versus seed
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High in grape berry skin and in high-PA transgenic poplar showing elevated PD
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Accessory cofactor at early hydroxylation branch point
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Low cytochrome b5 expression may explain failure of F3'5'H-only overexpression to alter PD in poplar.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Supports formation of trihydroxylated flavonoids indirectly
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Required cofactor for branch control but not itself established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Cytochrome b5 expression correlates with high PD accumulation. 2. It is a required coenzyme for F3'5'H. 3. Coexpression with F3'5'H is likely necessary to engineer PA hydroxylation.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Fruit skin and other PA-accumulating tissues
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports formation of hydroxylation patterns influencing taste and defense

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important companion gene because DFR substrate specificity can constrain hydroxylation engineering; should be matched with F3'5'H alleles during PA structure engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Influences which hydroxylated substrates can proceed toward flavan-3-ol and PA formation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Engineering success in rose through combined manipulation with F3'5'H; biochemical knowledge of substrate discrimination
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies immediate precursor pool for ANS/LAR/ANR branches
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Substrate specificity toward differently hydroxylated dihydroflavonols must be considered when shifting PAs toward PD-rich forms.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Core flavonoid trunk leading to anthocyanidins/flavan-3-ols
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May constrain intended structural engineering if incompatible with altered substrate pools.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; important substrate-selective control point
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DFR is part of the core flavonoid pathway leading to PA precursors. 2. Its substrate specificity affects success of hydroxylation engineering. 3. Combined DFR and F3'5'H manipulation can redirect flavonoid product profiles.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Controls precursor supply affecting PA composition

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: General plant flavonoid pathway; Medicago-related discussion
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Must be considered when manipulating LAR because ANS can reverse/redirect flux; potential target for balancing anthocyanin versus PA production.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts leucoanthocyanidins to anthocyanidins; additionally shown to catalyze catechin to cyanidin conversion
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Affects flux partitioning between anthocyanidin/anthocyanin and PA-related metabolites.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Referenced enzymatic evidence discussed in relation to Liu et al.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms ANR substrate and can compete with PA branch by redirecting flavan-3-ol intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Late trunk step at anthocyanidin/PA branch interface
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Unexpected ability to convert catechin to cyanidin adds complexity to attempts to engineer PA stereochemistry.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Anthocyanidin-forming branch feeding ANR and anthocyanins
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May undermine attempts to increase catechin-type PAs by consuming intermediates.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Anthocyanidins; cyanidin from catechin in side reaction
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ANS occupies a central branch position between anthocyanin and PA metabolism. 2. It supplies anthocyanidins for ANR. 3. Its additional conversion of catechin to cyanidin complicates engineering of PA stereochemistry.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Influences balance of anthocyanin and PA products

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Direct target to engineer galloylated PAs with altered taste and potentially improved health-promoting properties; likely requires coexpression of galloyl-glucose biosynthetic enzyme(s).
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers galloyl group from 1-O-galloyl-D-glucose to epicatechin, forming galloylated flavan-3-ols/PAs
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables PA galloylation, an important structural decoration associated with higher astringency and possible health benefits.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene cloning and enzyme identification in persimmon
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Not detailed beyond occurrence in persimmon fruit system
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Belongs to serine carboxypeptidase-like acyltransferase family; galloyl-glucose supply is also needed.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May increase astringency substantially; requires metabolic supply of galloyl donor.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A persimmon SCPL acyltransferase catalyzes galloylation of epicatechin. 2. This reaction underlies an important PA structural decoration. 3. The cloned gene offers a direct route to engineer galloylated PAs if galloyl donor supply is also ensured.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Persimmon fruit and other engineered PA tissues
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Influences astringency and may enhance health-promoting activity

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Not specified in this perspective; overexpression commonly used in transgenics
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Foundational PA MYB useful as reference regulator; demonstrates that PA pathways can be switched by single MYB genes, supporting ortholog-based engineering in crops.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activates PA biosynthesis in seed coat.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transparent testa mutant genetics; transcriptional regulatory studies; protein interaction studies with TT8/TTG1
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Master PA regulator in Arabidopsis seed coat and prototype for crop PA MYBs.
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Not specified for TT2 itself in this article
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: First MYB PA regulator identified; led to discovery of crop PA MYBs.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: MBW complex with TT8 and TTG1
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates PA pathway genes in seed coat
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TT2/MYB123 was the first PA-specific MYB identified. 2. It acts in an MBW complex with TT8 and TTG1 to activate seed-coat PA synthesis. 3. It established the regulatory paradigm for PA engineering in crops.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense, astringency, and health-related dietary polyphenol

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Not specified; overexpression in grape hairy roots
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong positive regulator suitable for boosting PA levels in fruit tissues; can be combined with bHLH partners for stronger induction.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strongly enhances PA accumulation when overexpressed.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression in grapevine hairy roots; expression studies in seed tissues
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major PA regulator active in grape seed, sufficient for robust PA induction.
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: MBW complex upregulates bHLH cofactor gene, generating positive feedback in grape
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: VvMYC1; WD40 partner(s) not named here
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Defines a distinct PA-MYB subgroup present also in cacao and poplar.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Very strong induction may require tissue targeting to avoid unwanted effects.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: MBW complex with bHLH and WD40 cofactors
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VvMYBPA1 is a major grape PA regulator in seeds. 2. Overexpression in hairy roots strongly elevates PA levels. 3. It functions in an MBW complex and contributes to positive feedback on PA regulation.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Affects wine/food astringency and health-related polyphenol content

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Not specified; overexpression in grape hairy roots
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering PA accumulation in grape skin or other fruit tissues; cross-species PA enhancement likely feasible with endogenous cofactors.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strongly enhances PA accumulation when overexpressed.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression in grapevine hairy roots; tissue expression analysis
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major PA regulator in grape skin and ortholog of Arabidopsis TT2.
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Positive feedback through MBW-mediated upregulation of bHLH cofactor gene in grape
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: VvMYC1; WD40 partner(s) not named here
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: MBW complex with bHLH and WD40 cofactors
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VvMYBPA2 is a major grape skin PA regulator. 2. It is orthologous to Arabidopsis TT2. 3. Overexpression strongly increases PA levels, highlighting utility for engineering.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to fruit quality, astringency, and health-promoting polyphenols

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Not specified; suppression/overexpression in transgenic persimmon callus
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potent activator for fruit PA engineering, but requires caution because ectopic expression caused toxicity, growth arrest, and blackening; best used with tissue-specific/inducible promoters.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Suppression reduces PA synthesis; overexpression induces PA accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic suppression and overexpression in persimmon callus; heterologous overexpression in kiwifruit callus
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major fruit PA regulator in persimmon.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Callus culture context influences toxicity and regeneration
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functions across species with endogenous cofactors, demonstrating cross-species utility.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Ectopic PA accumulation was toxic; calli stopped growing and turned black; prevented shoot regeneration in kiwifruit callus.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Likely MBW complex with endogenous bHLH and WD40 proteins
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DkMYB4 is the major persimmon fruit PA regulator. 2. Silencing reduces PA synthesis, while overexpression induces PAs. 3. Strong ectopic activation can be toxic, highlighting the need for controlled expression strategies.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to astringency and health-related fruit polyphenols

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species: Apple; Arabidopsis cells; poplar interaction context
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Malus domestica; Arabidopsis thaliana; Populus spp.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Cross-species compatible activator for apple PA biofortification and likely other crops; can interact with heterologous bHLH cofactors.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Functions as a PA regulator capable of inducing PA-related transcriptional programs.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Functional assays in Arabidopsis cells and interaction with poplar bHLH
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Demonstrates transferable PA-regulator function suitable for fruit engineering.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Apple PA-related tissues not specified in detail
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Highlights broad compatibility of PA MYBs across species, easing metabolic engineering.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdMYB9 is an apple PA regulator. 2. It functions in Arabidopsis cells and interacts with poplar bHLH, showing cross-species flexibility. 3. This makes it attractive for PA biofortification in fruit crops.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Health-promoting fruit polyphenol and sensory trait determinant

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Not specified; overexpressed transgenically in alfalfa and white clover
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species: Black clover source; applied in alfalfa and white clover
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Trifolium arvense; Medicago sativa; Trifolium repens
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Top candidate for engineering forage legumes with leaf PAs to improve ruminant health and reduce environmental impacts; field deployment is explicitly envisioned.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Stimulates strong PA accumulation in leaves of species that normally have little or no leaf PA.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic overexpression in alfalfa and white clover with metabolite quantification
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Highly effective leaf-PA activator for forage legumes.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Active in leaves when overexpressed; originally identified from black clover leaves
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: TT2-like MYB; more effective in leaves than some Medicago PA MYBs.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No major penalty emphasized here, but precision control still desirable.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Likely MBW complex with endogenous cofactors
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates PA biosynthetic genes in leaves
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaMYB14 regulates leaf PAs in black clover. 2. Overexpression induces ~1% DW PA in alfalfa and up to 1.8% DW in white clover leaves. 3. It is a leading gene for practical engineering of forage legumes.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Improves ruminant nutrition and animal/environmental health

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Not specified; ectopic expression in Medicago sativa roots
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species: Medicago trunculata genes tested in alfalfa roots/leaves
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can induce PA accumulation in roots, but weaker leaf activity limits utility for foliar forage engineering compared with TaMYB14.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to seed PA regulation and induces substantial PAs when ectopically expressed in alfalfa roots.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Ectopic expression in Medicago sativa roots/leaves
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition- and tissue-dependent PA activator rather than universally strong regulator.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Root versus leaf tissue context strongly affects outcome
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: More effective in roots than leaves
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Seed PA regulation in Medicago trunculata
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Less active in leaves than TaMYB14.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Lower efficacy in leaves may limit practical deployment.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates PA pathway genes, especially in roots
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MtMYB14 contributes to seed PA regulation in Medicago trunculata. 2. It can induce substantial PAs in alfalfa roots when ectopically expressed. 3. Its weaker leaf activity reduces value for leaf-PA engineering.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Not specified; ectopic expression in Medicago sativa roots
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species: Medicago trunculata genes tested in alfalfa roots/leaves
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for root/seed PA studies, but less effective for leaf engineering than TaMYB14.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to seed PA regulation and induces substantial PAs in alfalfa roots when ectopically expressed.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Ectopic expression assays in Medicago sativa roots/leaves
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Greater effect in roots than leaves
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates PA genes mainly in root/seed contexts
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MtMYB5 contributes to Medicago seed PA regulation. 2. Ectopic expression induces PAs in alfalfa roots. 3. It is less effective in leaves, limiting some engineering uses.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Not specified; ectopic expression in Medicago sativa roots
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species: Medicago trunculata genes tested in alfalfa roots/leaves
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful PA activator for root/seed systems; less effective for leaf bioengineering than TaMYB14.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to regulation of seed PAs and induces substantial PAs in alfalfa roots upon ectopic expression.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Contributes positively but with tissue specificity.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Stronger root effect than leaf effect
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included among forage legume PA-acting MYBs.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates PA genes in root/seed contexts
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MtPAR contributes to PA regulation in Medicago. 2. It can induce substantial PAs in alfalfa roots. 3. Its lower activity in leaves reduces value for foliar engineering.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Constitutive overexpression in transgenic poplar (promoter not specified)
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Very potent activator for boosting vegetative-tissue PAs; useful for engineering but should be monitored for impacts on other phenolics and insect interactions.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression increases leaf PA content 40- to 50-fold in greenhouse-grown poplars.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major activator of leaf PA synthesis in poplar.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse-grown leaves; stress-induced PA context also discussed
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Broadly expressed in leaves and vegetative tissues
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Mutual feed-forward regulation with MYB115; MBW also can enhance bHLH cofactor expression
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: TT8-like bHLH; WD40 cofactors; MYB115 promoter
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Overlapping function with MYB115; strong pathway activation can reduce other phenolics and affect herbivory.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Unexpected reduction in other phenolics and increased palatability to insect pests when PA pathway is strongly enhanced.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: PA biosynthetic genes; bHLH cofactor gene; MYB115 promoter
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Strong activation of PA genes and reinforcement of PA regulatory network
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MYB134 is a major poplar leaf PA activator. 2. It and MYB115 regulate each other's promoters, creating feed-forward amplification. 3. Overexpression causes dramatic PA accumulation but can perturb broader phenolic metabolism.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense against herbivores and ecological interactions
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Vegetative development; stress-induced PA synthesis context

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Constitutive overexpression in transgenic poplar (promoter not specified)
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potent activator for vegetative PA engineering, but pathway-wide effects on other phenolics require monitoring.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression greatly elevates PA accumulation in leaves.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major overlapping activator of poplar leaf PA synthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: TT8-like bHLH; WD40 cofactors; MYB134 promoter
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: MYBPA1-type regulator overlapping with MYB134 function.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Strong enhancement can reduce other phenolics and increase insect palatability indirectly.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Strong activation of PA pathway genes
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MYB115 is a broad poplar leaf PA regulator. 2. It participates in mutual promoter activation with MYB134, amplifying PA biosynthesis. 3. Overexpression strongly induces PAs but can alter broader phenolic balance.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Not specified; functional studies including transgenic overexpression
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful cofactor for stacking with PA MYBs to maximize PA induction; alone gives only modest PA increases.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as bHLH cofactor enabling PA-MBW function; overexpression alone modestly increases PAs.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Protein interaction/functional complex assays; transgenic overexpression
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential cofactor but less pathway-specific than MYB activators.
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Part of positive feedback loop because MBW upregulates bHLH cofactor gene in grape
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Transcriptional control of PA and anthocyanin biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functionally similar to Arabidopsis TT8 and can support either PA or anthocyanin pathway depending on MYB partner.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broad action across flavonoid branches may affect anthocyanins as well as PAs.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Flavonoid pathway genes as part of MBW complexes
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Supports activation of PA or anthocyanin genes depending on MYB partner
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VvMYC1 is the grape bHLH partner of the PA MBW complex. 2. It interacts with VvMYBPA1 for PA regulation and with VvMYBA1 for anthocyanin regulation. 3. Alone it has modest effects, but with MYBs it is useful for engineering.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Controls fruit polyphenol content and quality
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: MBW-network positive feedback and developmental cues

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: General cofactor required for PA engineering in Arabidopsis-like systems, but limited specificity makes it less attractive than MYB activators as a standalone target.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required bHLH partner for TT2-mediated seed coat PA synthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genetic and interaction studies in Arabidopsis
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential cofactor for Arabidopsis PA MBW complex.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: PA and anthocyanin transcriptional programs depending on MYB partner
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Shared cofactor between PA and anthocyanin pathways.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Manipulation may affect multiple flavonoid branches.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Flavonoid pathway genes as part of MBW complex
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Supports activation of PA genes with TT2 and anthocyanin genes with PAP1
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TT8 is the Arabidopsis bHLH cofactor for TT2-dependent PA regulation. 2. It also works with PAP1 in anthocyanin-producing cells. 3. This illustrates shared cofactors and MYB-determined specificity in flavonoid regulation.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense and seed coat polyphenol accumulation

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Core stabilizing cofactor in MBW complexes; useful support component but not pathway-specific enough for primary engineering target.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Stabilizes MYB-bHLH interaction in PA MBW complex.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential stabilizing cofactor enabling PA-specific MYB action.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed coat and anthocyanin-producing cell contexts
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: PA and anthocyanin transcriptional regulation as MBW component
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Specificity is determined mainly by MYB rather than TTG1.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broad regulatory involvement beyond PA pathway
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Flavonoid genes indirectly via MBW complexes
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TTG1 is the WD40 component of the Arabidopsis PA MBW complex. 2. It stabilizes interaction between MYB and bHLH proteins. 3. It is necessary for PA activation but does not itself confer pathway specificity.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Not specified; overexpression in grape hairy roots
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for suppressing negative regulation to enhance PA flux; may also help tune competing phenylpropanoid pathways.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression downregulates PAs and other phenylpropanoids.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Strong repressor of PA and broader phenylpropanoid metabolism.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Negative control of PA and phenylpropanoid biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of the first R2R3-MYB repressors linked to PA metabolism.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Repression decreases beneficial PA accumulation; manipulating it may have wide phenylpropanoid effects.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: PA biosynthetic genes and other phenylpropanoid genes
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Acts through promoter binding and bHLH interaction
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VvMYBC2-L1 is a grape MYB repressor affecting PA metabolism. 2. Overexpression suppresses PAs and other phenylpropanoids. 3. Repressor manipulation may offer additional engineering leverage.

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate target for gene editing/downregulation to expand or enhance seed PA accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Restricts spatial distribution of PAs in seed coat.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Negative control of seed PA biosynthesis/spatial patterning
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussed as an alfalfa repressor restricting PA distribution in seed coat.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Downregulation may alter spatial patterning and potentially other phenylpropanoids.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: PA biosynthetic genes in seed coat
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Alfalfa MYB2 acts as a negative regulator of seed PA accumulation. 2. It may define where PAs accumulate within the seed coat. 3. Removing such repression could be useful in engineering.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Not specified; overexpression in transgenic poplar
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Editing or downregulating this repressor could enhance leaf PAs, but broader effects on anthocyanins and phenylpropanoids must be considered.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression reduces both PAs and anthocyanins.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Negative regulator that likely dampens stress-induced PA accumulation in leaves.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Stress-induced PA synthesis context in leaves
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaves; linked to stress-induced PA dynamics
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Negative regulation of PA and anthocyanin biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Represents broader target range typical of MYB repressors.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Manipulation may also affect anthocyanins and other phenolics.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Acts through promoter binding and bHLH interaction
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Suppresses PA and anthocyanin pathway genes
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Poplar MYB182 represses PA accumulation in leaves. 2. Overexpression lowers both PAs and anthocyanins. 3. It may function to limit stress-induced PA production and is a candidate for derepression-based engineering.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species: Strawberry repressor applied in Lotus corniculatus
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Demonstrates that MYB repressors can be used transgenically to reduce PA content when lower tannin levels are desired.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Used to reduce PA contents in Lotus corniculatus.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Proof-of-concept repressor for lowering PA accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Negative control of anthocyanin/PA-related flavonoid pathways
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Originally characterized as anthocyanin repressor, but can suppress PA accumulation in a heterologous system.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May broadly repress flavonoid pathways beyond PAs.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FaMYB1 is a flavonoid repressor first studied in strawberry. 2. It can also reduce PA levels in Lotus corniculatus. 3. This demonstrates the utility of MYB repressors as tools to lower PA accumulation.
